question: Project Narrative
complianceGuidance: The Project Narrative must be attached in the designated section of the application. The User Guide requires that the Project Narrative be uploaded by clicking the Attach File button in the Project Narrative form. The narrative must address all required elements as outlined in the NOFO and User Guide, and must not exceed the page limit specified in the NOFO. The document should be complete, legible, and address the applicant’s proposed project, including service area, population, and approach.
description: Check that the Project Narrative is attached and meets content and format requirements.

question: Attachment 6: Co-Applicant Agreement (required for new public center applicants that have a co-applicant board)
complianceGuidance: Attachment 6 is required only for public center applicants that have a co-applicant board. The User Guide specifies that this attachment must be uploaded if applicable, and only one file is allowed. The agreement must be current, signed, and demonstrate the relationship and roles between the public entity and the co-applicant board. If not applicable, the applicant should indicate N/A.
description: Check that the Co-Applicant Agreement is attached for new public center applicants with a co-applicant board.

question: Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Center Status (new applicants)
lookFor: Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Center Status
complianceGuidance: Attachment 11 is required for new applicants and must provide documentation of nonprofit or public center status, such as IRS determination letters, articles of incorporation, or government documentation. The User Guide requires that this attachment be uploaded if applicable, and only one file is allowed. The document must be current and clearly demonstrate the applicant’s eligibility as a nonprofit or public entity.
description: Check that evidence of nonprofit or public center status is attached for new applicants.

question: Is the applicant organization a domestic public or private nonprofit entity, including faith-based, and community-based organizations, Tribes, and tribal organizations?
suggestedResources: Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Center Status
lookFor: Attachment 11: Evidence of Nonprofit or Public Center Status
complianceGuidance: The User Guide requires that the applicant be a domestic public or private nonprofit entity, including faith-based and community-based organizations, Tribes, and tribal organizations. Evidence must be provided in Attachment 11, which should include official documentation such as IRS determination letters, articles of incorporation, or government documentation. The documentation must clearly show the applicant’s status as a domestic public or private nonprofit entity.
description: Verify applicant is a domestic public or private nonprofit entity based on documentation.

question: Does the applicant propose to perform a substantive role in the project (i.e., does not apply on behalf of another organization)?
suggestedResources: SF-424A | Project Narrative | Budget Narrative | Attachment 2: Bylaws | Attachment 3: Project Organizational Chart | Form 5A: Services Provided | Form 8: Health Center Agreements
complianceGuidance: The User Guide requires that the applicant organization must perform a substantive role in the project and not apply on behalf of another organization. The Project Narrative, Bylaws, Organizational Chart, and Forms 5A and 8 must demonstrate that the applicant is responsible for project oversight, service delivery, and programmatic and fiscal management. If Form 8 or other documents indicate that another organization is the primary operator or decision-maker, the applicant is not eligible.
description: Verify the applicant is not a pass-through and will perform a substantive role in the project.

question: Does the applicant propose to operate a health center that makes all required primary health care services available and accessible in the service area, either directly or through established arrangements, without regard for ability to pay? (Note that applicants may not propose to provide ONLY a single service or any subset of the required primary health care services.)
complianceGuidance: The User Guide requires that applicants must make all required primary health care services available and accessible in the service area, either directly or through formal arrangements, and without regard for ability to pay. The Project Narrative must describe how all required services will be provided and must not propose only a single service or a subset of required services. The application must demonstrate compliance with the Health Center Program’s comprehensive service requirements.
description: Verify applicant proposes to provide all required primary health care services, not just a subset.

question: Does the applicant propose to make General Primary Medical Care available directly (Column I) and/or through formal written contractual agreements in which the health center pays for the service (Column II)?
complianceGuidance: The User Guide specifies that General Primary Medical Care must be made available either directly (Column I) or through formal written contractual agreements (Column II) in which the health center pays for the service. Form 5A must indicate at least one of these methods for General Primary Medical Care. If neither Column I nor II is selected for this service, the application is not compliant.
description: Check Form 5A for General Primary Medical Care in Column I and/or II.

question: Does the applicant propose to provide access to services for all individuals in the service area and target population? If a sub-population is targeted (e.g., a school-based site that targets school-aged children), will health center services be available and accessible to others seeking services at the proposed site(s)?
suggestedResources: Project Narrative | Form 5A: Services Provided | Project Abstract
complianceGuidance: Evaluate whether the applicant's service model demonstrates accessibility to all individuals, not just a targeted sub-population. Consider the TOTALITY of evidence across multiple sources: (1) Project Narrative describing populations served and service delivery model (e.g., fixed sites, mobile units, outreach), (2) Form 5A showing full-scope required services provided, (3) Project Abstract describing target populations served. If a sub-population is targeted (e.g., school-based sites for school-aged children), answer 'Yes' if the evidence shows services extend beyond that sub-population — such as serving families, homeless individuals, public housing residents, community members, or providing mobile/outreach services to the broader community. Answer 'No' ONLY if the application restricts services exclusively to a single sub-population with no indication of broader access to others seeking care at the proposed site(s).
description: Verify services are accessible to all in the service area, not just a sub-population.

question: Does the applicant propose a service area announced under this NOFO number?
suggestedResources: Summary Page | SAAT | Abstract | Project Narrative | Form 5B: Service Sites
complianceGuidance: The User Guide requires that the proposed service area must match a service area announced under the current NOFO number, as listed in the Service Area Announcement Table (SAAT). The application’s Summary Page, Abstract, Project Narrative, and Form 5B must reference a valid SAAT Service Area ID, city, and state. If the service area is not listed in the current SAAT, the application is not eligible.
description: Verify the proposed service area matches a SAAT service area for this NOFO.

question: Is the total number of unduplicated patients projected to be served in 2025 on Form 1A at least 75 percent of the SAAT Patient Target?
complianceGuidance: The User Guide states that the unduplicated patient projection for calendar year 2025 on Form 1A must be at least 75 percent of the Patient Target listed in the SAAT for the proposed service area. The system should compare the total projected patients to the SAAT Patient Target and confirm the 75% threshold is met. If the projection is below 75%, the application is not eligible.
description: Check that projected patients on Form 1A are at least 75% of the SAAT Patient Target.

question: Does the applicant propose to serve patients for each Service Type listed in the SAAT?
complianceGuidance: The User Guide requires that the applicant must project patients for each Service Type listed for the service area in the SAAT. Form 1A must include projections for all required Service Types. Zeros are acceptable if not applicable, but all Service Types must be addressed. The application must not omit any Service Type listed in the SAAT.
description: Verify all SAAT Service Types are addressed in Form 1A patient projections.

question: Does the applicant request annual SAC funding that does NOT exceed the SAAT Total Funding for the service area?
complianceGuidance: The User Guide specifies that the total annual SAC funding requested in the SF-424A must not exceed the Total Funding amount listed in the SAAT for the service area. The system should compare the total federal funding requested to the SAAT maximum. If the request exceeds the SAAT amount, the application is not eligible.
description: Check that total SAC funding requested does not exceed the SAAT maximum.

question: Does the applicant propose to serve all currently targeted population types (i.e., CHC, MHC, HCH, PHPC) from the SAAT?
complianceGuidance: The User Guide requires that the applicant must propose to serve all population types (CHC, MHC, HCH, PHPC) that have a funding amount greater than $0 in the SAAT for the service area. The SF-424A must include funding requests for each applicable population type. If any targeted population type is omitted, the application is not eligible.
description: Verify all population types with SAAT funding are included in the application.

question: Does the applicant maintain the current funding distribution (i.e., CHC, MHC, HCH, PHPC) from the SAAT?
complianceGuidance: The User Guide states that the funding distribution across population types (CHC, MHC, HCH, PHPC) must be maintained as listed in the SAAT for the service area. The SF-424A must reflect the same proportional distribution of funding as the SAAT. If the distribution is not maintained, the application is not eligible.
description: Check that funding distribution by population type matches the SAAT.

question: New or competing supplement applicant: Is at least one new full-time (operational 40 hours or more per week) permanent, fixed building site with a verifiable street address proposed on Form 5B? *If funding is only requested for MHC, the applicant may propose a new full-time seasonal (rather than permanent) service delivery site.
complianceGuidance: The User Guide requires that new or competing supplement applicants must propose at least one new Service Delivery or Administrative/Service Delivery site on Form 5B. For CHC, HCH, or PHPC funding, the site must be permanent, fixed, and operational at least 40 hours per week with a verifiable street address. If only MHC funding is requested, a full-time seasonal site is acceptable. The site must not be admin-only.
description: Check for at least one new full-time permanent (or seasonal for MHC) site on Form 5B.

question: New or competing supplement applicant: For Form 5B: Service Sites, does the applicant include: · Service area zip codes where the zip code patient percentages on the SAAT total at least 75 percent; or . All service area zip codes if the sum of all zip code patient percentages on the SAAT is less than 75 percent?
complianceGuidance: The User Guide requires that Form 5B must include service area zip codes such that the sum of zip code patient percentages from the SAAT is at least 75 percent. If the total of all zip code percentages in the SAAT is less than 75 percent, all zip codes must be included. The system should compare the zip codes listed in Form 5B to the SAAT and confirm compliance.
description: Check that Form 5B zip codes cover at least 75% of SAAT patient percentages or all zip codes if total is less than 75%.

question: New or competing supplement applicant requesting PHPC funding: Does the applicant demonstrate consultation with residents of public housing in the preparation of the SAC application, and ensure ongoing consultation with the residents regarding the planning and administration of the health center?
complianceGuidance: The User Guide requires that applicants requesting PHPC funding must demonstrate consultation with residents of public housing in preparing the application and ensure ongoing consultation regarding planning and administration. The Project Narrative must describe these consultation activities. If not addressed, the application is not compliant.
description: Check for evidence of public housing resident consultation in the Project Narrative for PHPC applicants.

question: New or competing supplement applicant requesting PHPC and/or PHPC funding: Does the applicant attest on the Summary Page that funding will be utilized to supplement, and not supplant, the expenditures of the health center and the value of in-kind contributions for the delivery of services to these populations?
complianceGuidance: The User Guide requires that applicants requesting PHPC and/or HCH funding must attest on the Summary Page that grant funds will supplement, not supplant, health center expenditures and in-kind contributions for these populations. The attestation must be checked and, if required, a description provided. If not attested, the application is not compliant.
description: Check for required supplement/not supplant attestation on the Summary Page for PHPC/HCH applicants.

question: If the Form 1A patient projection is less than 95% of the SAAT Patient Target, is the annual SAC funding request reduced accordingly in the SF-424A and Budget Narrative? *This question does not impact application completeness or eligibility.
suggestedResources: SF-424A | Budget Narrative | SAAT | Patient and Budget Calculator
complianceGuidance: The User Guide and NOFO require that if the Form 1A patient projection is less than 95% of the SAAT Patient Target, the annual SAC funding request in the SF-424A and Budget Narrative must be reduced proportionally. The system should verify that the funding request is adjusted in accordance with the patient projection percentage. This check is for informational purposes and does not affect eligibility.
description: Check that funding request is reduced if patient projection is less than 95% of SAAT target.

question: Is the application complete based on the required documents mentioned in the Completeness Checklist?
complianceGuidance: The User Guide requires that all documents listed in the Completeness Checklist (including all required forms and attachments) are submitted and meet the content requirements. The application is considered complete if all required items are present, properly labeled, and meet the criteria outlined in the User Guide. Missing or incomplete documents result in an incomplete application.
description: Check that all required documents and forms are present and complete.

question: Based on BPHC OPPD review, is the applicant eligible?
complianceGuidance: Eligibility is determined based on the cumulative results of all prior compliance checks, including applicant type, substantive role, service area, patient projections, funding requests, and all other eligibility requirements as outlined in the User Guide. The applicant is eligible if all required criteria are met and no disqualifying issues are identified.
description: Final eligibility determination based on all prior compliance checks.
